details: The “Nishulk Ganavesh Yojana” is a scheme under the Samagra Shiksha Programme, implemented by the Secondary Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. The scheme aims to provide free uniforms to all girl students studying in Classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, as well as to all boys belonging to Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes, and boys from the General category who fall under the Below Poverty Line category.
benefits: Under this scheme, two sets of uniforms per student are provided through DBT/SMC to students studying in classes 1 to 8 of government and government-aided schools, covering all girls, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe categories, and boys from the General category belonging to BPL families.
eligibility: The applicant must be a resident of Uttarakhand.
The applicant must be either a girl student, or a boy student belonging to the Scheduled Caste, Scheduled Tribe, or General Category BPL family.
The applicant must be studying in Classes 1 to 8.

offline: There is no selection process; all eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 are provided free uniforms or the corresponding financial assistance.

documents_required: Documents are required as per the guidelines of the respective school.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the scheme?
faqs.answer: All girl students of Classes 1 to 8, all boys belonging to Scheduled Caste (SC) and Scheduled Tribe (ST) categories, and boys from General Category BPL families studying in government and government-aided schools are eligible.

faqs.question: Which classes are covered under the scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme covers students studying in Classes 1 to 8.

faqs.question: How many sets of uniforms are provided under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Each eligible student receives two sets of uniforms every year.

faqs.question: Is there any selection process for the scheme?
faqs.answer: No, there is no selection process. All eligible students from Classes 1 to 8 receive uniforms or related financial assistance.

faqs.question: Is the scheme applicable only to government schools?
faqs.answer: The scheme is applicable to students studying in government and government-aided schools.

faqs.question: Are private school students eligible under this scheme?
